methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Aeon Industrial is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Naweed Tahmas and Joe Cieslik that has raised $18.6M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms spanning Seed and Series A stages, including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $18.6M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $46M. Peer-median analysis examines five same-sector comparables: Tenna Systems at $60M (Seed), NODA AI at $125M (Series A), Ares Industries at $150M (Series A), Vatn Systems at $300M (Series A), and Stark Defence at $500M (Series A). The median valuation across this cohort is $150M, with a range of $60M–$500M.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $40M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $98M. The wide dispersion between methods reflects meaningful uncertainty around Aeon's positioning within the Defense & Security peer set. Key variables driving final valuation include relative product maturity, customer traction, and competitive differentiation versus the identified peer cohort—factors not quantifiable from current data.
